install.stdout:

Tool 'nugraph' (version '0.4.0') was successfully installed.

opencli.stderr:
Error: Unknown command 'opencli'.
       cli opencli
           ^^^^^^^ No such command
DESCRIPTION:
Generates dependency graphs for .NET projects and NuGet packages
USAGE:
    nugraph [SOURCE] [OPTIONS]
EXAMPLES:
    nugraph spectre.console/src/Spectre.Console.Cli/Spectre.Console.Cli.csproj --include-version
    nugraph Serilog.Sinks.MSSqlServer --ignore Microsoft.Data.SqlClient --ignore "System.*"
    nugraph Newtonsoft.Json/12.0.3 --framework netstandard1.0
    nugraph Azure.Core --direction TopToBottom --output Azure.Core.gv
    nugraph Polly --format dot --title ""
ARGUMENTS:
    [SOURCE]    The source of the graph                                                                         
                Can be either                                                                                   
                  * the name of a NuGet package, optionally with a specific version, e.g. Newtonsoft.Json/13.0.3
                  * a .NET project file (csproj/fsproj/vbproj)                                                  
                  * a directory containing a single .NET project                                                
                                                                                                                
OPTIONS:
                                          DEFAULT                                                                                                                                                                                                 
    -h, --help                                                            Prints help information                                                                                                                                                 
    -v, --version                                                         Prints version information                                                                                                                                              
    -f, --framework <FRAMEWORK>                                           The target framework to consider when building the dependency graph                                                                                                     
                                                                          See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/frameworks#supported-target-frameworks for the list of supported target frameworks                                
    -r, --runtime <RUNTIME_IDENTIFIER>                                    The target runtime to consider when building the dependency graph                                                                                                       
                                                                          See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/rid-catalog#known-rids for the list of known runtime identifiers                                                      
    -o, --output <OUTPUT>                                                 The path to the dependency graph output file                                                                                                                            
                                                                          A Mermaid diagram is written if the file name ends with either .mmd or .mermaid, otherwise a Graphviz diagram is written                                                
                                                                          If not specified, the default web browser is used to render the dependency graph using an online service                                                                
                                                                          See also the --format and --url options                                                                                                                                 
    -m, --format <FORMAT>                 mermaid                         The format to use when the --output option is not specified                                                                                                             
                                                                          Use mmd or mermaid for Mermaid Live Editor https://mermaid.live                                                                                                         
                                                                          Use dot, gv or graphviz for Edotor https://edotor.net                                                                                                                   
                                                                          See https://github.com/0xced/nugraph/#output for even more supported formats                                                                                            
    -u, --url                             open                            Defines what to do when the --output option is not specified                                                                                                            
                                                                          Use open (the default) for rendering the dependency graph using an online service or print for printing the graph URL on the standard output without opening the browser
                                                                          Both options can be combined with a comma, i.e. open,print to both open the URL and print it                                                                            
    -d, --direction <GRAPH_DIRECTION>     LeftToRight                     The direction of the dependency graph, possible values are LeftToRight (recommended for large graphs) and TopToBottom (good for small graphs)                           
    -t, --title <GRAPH_TITLE>             Dependency graph of [SOURCE]    The title of the dependency graph                                                                                                                                       
    -s, --include-version                                                 Include package versions in the dependency graph nodes, e.g. Serilog/4.3.0 instead of Serilog                                                                           
    -i, --ignore <PACKAGE>                                                Packages to ignore in the dependency graph, may be used multiple times and supports * wildcards, e.g. --ignore "System.*"                                               
        --no-links                                                        Remove clickable links from the the dependency graph                                                                                                                    
                                                                          Can be useful to reduce the size of the graph if it becomes too large and Mermaid Live Editor is returning "Maximum text size in diagram exceeded"                      
    -l, --log <LEVEL>                     Warning                         The NuGet operations log level, possible values are Debug, Verbose, Information, Minimal, Warning and Error
